_note: Catalogue of the major damaging frost events in the Brazilian arabica belt. Used by scraper.rules.frost_model's backtest/sanity harness to check the severity ladder classifies the historic disasters as 'critical', and by the frontend frost view for historical context. `observed_air_min_c` is a REPRESENTATIVE 2 m air minimum reported for the worst-hit region on the peak night (screen-height; the canopy/grass minimum ran several deg colder). `mechanism` is from the meteorological record of the event. `frost_areas` pins each event to the SPECIFIC municipalities the historical record reports as frost-hit (the 'appointed frost area'), with lat/lon, so the blind backtest samples where the damage actually occurred rather than a regional-mean centroid that averages the valley cold-pool away. The blind ERA5 backtest (scraper.backfill_frost_backtest --mode events, on GitHub Actions where archive-api.open-meteo.com is reachable) samples those points from ERA5-Land (~9 km — it resolves the cold-pooling that the ~31 km ERA5 grid smooths out) and checks the ladder fires 'critical'. Coordinates are the reported frost municipalities; where even ERA5-Land reads mild at a point, the reanalysis still under-resolves that specific hollow — not a threshold error, and never a reason to loosen the ladder.

events.label: Geada Negra (Black Frost)

frost_areas.note: Epicentre; ~900 M trees killed. Recorded -3.5 C air.
Southern SP near the PR border, hard-hit in 1975.
SW Sul de Minas — the northern reach of the 1975 air mass; Paraná was the epicentre.

events.crop_impact: Catastrophic: on the order of 70% of Brazil's coffee trees killed; permanent collapse of Paraná as the leading producing state. Arabica prices roughly tripled through 1976-77. A dry hard freeze under a polar air mass — literally the 'black frost'.
events.source: Widely documented; the benchmark Brazilian frost disaster. Londrina -3.5 C; parts of SP -3 C.

events.label: 1994 double frost

frost_areas.note: First frost (26-27 Jun) hit PR/SP hardest.
Alta Mogiana; hit in both waves.
Cooxupé heartland; the second (July) frost pushed north into Sul de Minas / Cerrado.

events.crop_impact: Two frosts ~two weeks apart devastated the 1995 crop; the worst since 1975. Arabica prices spiked well over 100%.
events.source: Widely documented; drove the mid-1990s price spike. Marengo et al. (1997), Mon. Wea. Rev., analyse the 26 Jun 1994 cold surge.

events.label: July 2021 frosts

frost_areas.note: Among the hardest-hit 2021 municipalities; also Tres Pontas, Monte Santo de Minas, Tres Coracoes.
Norte Pioneiro / northern PR belt frosted 29-30 Jul.
Cerrado Mineiro sits at the northern edge — marginal; the 2021 damage concentrated further south.

events.crop_impact: Strong polar air mass; clear, calm radiative nights drove canopy minima well below the ~-1.5 C screen minima. ~10-20% of the 2022/23 crop damaged; arabica futures roughly doubled over the following months.
events.source: Well documented; the most damaging frost in decades. Minas as low as -1.2 C; towns Ibiraci, Tres Pontas, Monte Santo de Minas, Tres Coracoes; SP Mogiana (Franca).
